title: Calathea leuconeura
taxonomy: Calathea leuconeura is classified as a species within the genus Calathea, which belongs to the family Marantaceae. The Marantaceae family is a group of flowering plants commonly known as the prayer plant family. This family consists of about 530 species, with Calathea being a popular and diverse genus within it. Calathea leuconeura is native to the tropical regions of South America, where it grows in shaded, humid environments. Its classification also places it within the order Zingiberales, a group of flowering plants that are mostly found in tropical regions.
uses: Calathea leuconeura has been used in traditional medicine for various purposes, including treating digestive issues and skin conditions. In some cultures, the plant's leaves are used to prepare a tea that helps alleviate symptoms of fever and inflammation.
